Player.title: Player
Player.description: Represents a player account in the Axiom Frontier game, holding their core profile information and game-related state.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Player entity.

username.description: The player's unique display name in the game.

email.description: The player's contact email address. (Not used for authentication).

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the player's account was created.

lastLoginAt.description: Timestamp indicating the player's last login.

currentPosition.description: A string representation of the player's current location within the game world (e.g., 'zoneId:x:y:z').

currencyAmount.description: The total amount of in-game currency currently held by the player.

inventoryItemIds.description: References to Item entities representing items in the player's inventory. (Relationship: Player 1:N Item instances, or N:N with Item if referring to item types)

Agent.title: Agent
Agent.description: Represents an autonomous in-game agent owned by a player, performing various actions like gathering, combat, or exploration.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Agent entity.

playerId.description: Reference to the Player who owns this agent. (Relationship: Player 1:N Agent)

name.description: The display name of the agent.

type.description: The functional type of the agent (e.g., 'Gatherer', 'Combatant', 'Explorer').

currentLocation.description: A string representation of the agent's current location within the game world.

status.description: The current operational status of the agent (e.g., 'idle', 'gathering', 'combat', 'traveling').

health.description: The current health points of the agent.

energy.description: The current energy points of the agent, consumed by actions.

experiencePoints.description: The accumulated experience points for the agent, contributing to its progression.

assignedQuestIds.description: References to Quest entities that this agent is currently assigned to. (Relationship: Agent N:N Quest)

WorldState.title: WorldState
WorldState.description: Stores global game parameters and dynamic world data, such as the overall civilization index, influencing game mechanics.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WorldState entity (expected to be a singleton, e.g., 'global_state').

civilizationIndex.description: The current global civilization index, reflecting the world's progress or state.

economyInflationRate.description: The current inflation rate affecting item prices and currency value in the game economy.

lastHeartbeat.description: Timestamp of the last scheduled 'world heartbeat' update.

currentSeason.description: The current in-game season or cyclical period.

gameParameters.description: A JSON string or other serialized format for additional dynamic game settings and parameters.

Item.title: Item
Item.description: Represents any purchasable, lootable, or craftable item within the game world, defining its properties and value.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Item entity.

name.description: The display name of the item.

description.description: A detailed textual description of the item and its use or lore.

type.description: The category or classification of the item (e.g., 'weapon', 'resource', 'consumable', 'currency').

baseValue.description: The base monetary value of the item in the game's primary currency.

weight.description: The weight of the item, impacting inventory capacity.

isStackable.description: Indicates whether this item can be stacked in inventory.

iconAssetId.description: Reference to the Asset entity representing the item's icon. (Relationship: Asset 1:N Item)

modelAssetId.description: Reference to the Asset entity representing the item's 3D model. (Relationship: Asset 1:N Item)

Quest.title: Quest
Quest.description: Represents a dynamic quest generated by the AI, offering objectives and rewards to players or their agents.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Quest entity.

description.description: A detailed textual description of the quest objectives and narrative.

status.description: The current status of the quest (e.g., 'available', 'active', 'completed', 'failed').

generatedByAI.description: Indicates whether this quest was dynamically generated by the AI system.

rewardItemIds.description: References to Item entities awarded upon successful completion of the quest.

rewardCurrencyAmount.description: The amount of in-game currency awarded upon completion.

requiredItemIds.description: References to Item entities required in the player's or agent's inventory to complete the quest.

assignedAgentIds.description: References to Agent entities currently undertaking this quest. (Relationship: Agent N:N Quest)

targetNpcId.description: Optional reference to an NPC entity relevant to this quest, such as a quest giver or target. (Relationship: NPC 1:N Quest)

NPC.title: NPC
NPC.description: Represents a non-player character with unique personalities and roles in the game world, potentially involved in quests or interactions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the NPC entity.

name.description: The display name of the NPC.

personalityDescription.description: An AI-generated textual description of the NPC's personality traits and characteristics.

dialogueTreeData.description: A serialized data structure (e.g., JSON string) defining the NPC's dialogue options and flow.

currentLocation.description: A string representation of the NPC's current location within the game world.

role.description: The primary role or function of the NPC (e.g., 'merchant', 'questgiver', 'adversary').

modelAssetId.description: Reference to the Asset entity representing the NPC's 3D model. (Relationship: Asset 1:N NPC)

factionId.description: Optional reference to the Faction entity this NPC belongs to. (Relationship: Faction 1:N NPC)

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Records details of secure e-commerce purchases made by players in the Axiom Storefront.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transaction entity.

playerId.description: Reference to the Player who made the transaction. (Relationship: Player 1:N Transaction)

transactionDate.description: Timestamp indicating when the transaction occurred.

amountPaid.description: The actual real-world currency amount paid for the transaction.

currencyCode.description: The ISO 4217 currency code of the amount paid (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

status.description: The current status of the transaction (e.g., 'pending', 'completed', 'failed', 'refunded').

purchasedItemIds.description: References to Item entities purchased in this transaction.

purchasedCurrencyAmount.description: The amount of in-game currency acquired through this transaction, if applicable.

paymentGatewayTransactionId.description: The unique transaction identifier provided by the external payment gateway (e.g., PayPal).

Asset.title: Asset
Asset.description: Manages references to game assets (3D models, textures, icons, audio) stored in Firebase Storage, used for dynamic loading by the Godot client.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Asset entity.

name.description: A descriptive name for the asset, for internal identification.

type.description: The category or format of the asset (e.g., 'model', 'texture', 'icon', 'audio', 'shader').

storagePath.description: The path or URI within Firebase Storage where the actual asset file is located.

version.description: The version identifier of the asset, for managing updates.

checksum.description: A checksum (e.g., MD5, SHA256 hash) for integrity verification of the asset file.

metadata.description: Optional serialized JSON string for additional asset-specific metadata (e.g., dimensions, poly count).

Faction.title: Faction
Faction.description: Represents a distinct group or organization within the game world, to which NPCs or players can belong, influencing relationships and interactions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Faction entity.

name.description: The official name of the faction.

description.description: A detailed description of the faction's goals, history, and characteristics.

alignment.description: The general moral or ethical alignment of the faction (e.g., 'neutral', 'friendly', 'hostile', 'lawful', 'chaotic').

leaderNpcId.description: Optional reference to the NPC entity identified as the leader of this faction. (Relationship: Faction 1:1 NPC)

definition.description: Stores the core profile and game-related state for each player. The `playerId` in the path matches the Firebase Authentication UID, ensuring path-based ownership. Read/write access is restricted to the owning player or administrators.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Player, corresponding to the Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Contains data for autonomous in-game agents owned by a specific player. Includes a denormalized 'playerId' field for authorization independence. Access is restricted to the owning player or administrators.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Player who owns this agent, derived from the parent collection path.

params.name: agentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Agent entity.

definition.description: Records secure e-commerce purchases made by a specific player. Includes a denormalized 'playerId' for authorization independence. Access is restricted to the owning player or administrators.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Player who made the transaction, derived from the parent collection path.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Transaction entity.

definition.description: Stores player-specific status and progress for global quests. While referencing the global Quest schema for core definitions, documents in this collection hold mutable, player-specific state (e.g., 'status': 'completed', 'progress': '5/10 objectives'). Includes denormalized 'playerId' and 'questId' for authorization independence.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Player to whom this quest status belongs, derived from the parent collection path.

params.name: questId
params.description: The unique identifier of the global Quest entity this status refers to, derived from the subcollection path.

definition.description: A singleton document holding global game parameters and dynamic world data. Read by all players; write access is restricted to administrators or trusted Cloud Functions (e.g., world heartbeat).
Defines all purchasable, lootable, or craftable item types in the game. These are global definitions. Read by all players; write access is restricted to administrators or trusted Cloud Functions.

params.name: itemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Item entity.

definition.description: Defines all dynamic quests generated by the AI. These are global quest definitions and their global state. Read by all players; write access is restricted to administrators or trusted Cloud Functions.

params.name: questId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Quest entity.

definition.description: Defines all non-player characters (NPCs) in the game world. Read by all players; write access is restricted to administrators or trusted Cloud Functions.

params.name: npcId
params.description: The unique identifier for the NPC entity.

definition.description: Manages references to game assets (3D models, textures, icons, audio) stored in Firebase Storage. Read by all players; write access is restricted to administrators or trusted Cloud Functions.

params.name: assetId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Asset entity.

definition.description: Defines all factions within the game world. Read by all players; write access is restricted to administrators or trusted Cloud Functions.

params.name: factionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Faction entity.

definition.description: A collection used for Database Access Control (DBAC) to identify administrative users. The mere existence of a document at this path for a given `userId` grants administrative privileges. Read access is restricted to admin users, write access to other admin users or super-admin functions.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (Firebase Authentication UID) who has been assigned the 'admin' role.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for Axiom Frontier is designed around strict access control, scalability, and debuggability, adhering to the core design principles of Authorization Independence and QAPs (Queries as Authorization Primitives).

**Authorization Independence:**
This structure prioritizes Authorization Independence by eliminating the need for `get()` calls in security rules for authorization checks. This is achieved through:
1.  **Path-Based Ownership:** Player-specific data, such as `/players/{playerId}`, `/players/{playerId}/agents/{agentId}`, `/players/{playerId}/transactions/{transactionId}`, and `/players/{playerId}/questStatuses/{questId}`, directly embeds the `playerId` (which corresponds to `request.auth.uid`) in the path. This allows security rules to instantly verify ownership (`request.auth.uid == playerId`) without needing to fetch a parent document or cross-reference other collections.
2.  **Denormalization of Owner IDs:** Although `playerId` is in the path for subcollections, `Agent` and `Transaction` documents explicitly include the `playerId` field. For `PlayerQuestStatus` documents, both `playerId` and `questId` are denormalized from the path into the document for clarity and simplified rule evaluation of individual documents. This ensures that a document carries its own authorization context.
3.  **Structural Segregation for Global Data:** Publicly accessible collections (`/items`, `/quests`, `/npcs`, `/assets`, `/factions`) are separated from private player data. These global collections do not rely on `request.auth.uid` for read authorization, ensuring their rules are simple ('allow read'). Write access to these global collections is restricted to administrators or trusted Cloud Functions, whose authorization is verified atomically using a dedicated admin roles collection (`/roles_admin/{userId}`).

**QAPs (Queries as Authorization Primitives):**
This design ensures that security rules are not used as filters and support efficient list operations:
1.  **Player-Owned Collections:** For private data like `/players/{playerId}/agents` or `/players/{playerId}/transactions`, a player can only query documents under their specific `playerId` path (i.e., `/players/{request.auth.uid}/agents`). The `list` operation in client-side queries is naturally constrained by the path, and security rules simply verify that the `playerId` in the path matches `request.auth.uid`. This eliminates the need for complex filtering logic within rules that would make `list` operations insecure or inefficient.
2.  **Global Collections:** Collections like `/items` and `/quests` are designed to be universally readable. Any `list` query on these collections is inherently secure because all documents within them are intended for public consumption (read-only). There's no requirement for user-specific filtering, thus adhering to QAPs.
3.  **Player Quest Progress:** By isolating player-specific quest progress into `/players/{playerId}/questStatuses/{questId}`, players can perform `list` operations to retrieve all quests assigned to them, along with their individual progress, without needing to filter a global `quests` collection based on complex ownership or assignment logic. This is critical for efficient client-side data fetching and robust security.

**Other Principles:**
*   **DBAC (Database Access Control):** Global roles are handled by the existence of a document in `/roles_admin/{userId}`, allowing for atomic checks.
*   **Clarity of Intent:** The clear distinction between `/players/{playerId}/...` and global collections, along with consistent naming (`playerId`, `agentId`), makes authorization intent explicit.
*   **Invariants:** Ownership is maintained through path-based structure and denormalization. Timestamps and denormalized data (e.g., `playerId` in `Agent`) are consistently stored within documents, supporting data integrity.
